place: Clifton Knolls Mill Creek, New York

facts.text: Clifton Knolls‑Mill Creek sits on a compact 1.64‑square‑mile parcel, mostly flat lowlands shaped by glacial deposits and subtle shale and sandstone layers.
The community’s name reflects its location near the tributary Dwaas Kill, which feeds wetlands and floodplains along the western edge of the CDP.
Owner‑occupied homes dominate Clifton Knolls‑Mill Creek, with 96% of units built in the 1990s and 2000s and a median home value of $371,300.
Historically, the area was part of the ancestral lands of the Mohawk and Mohican peoples, members of the Iroquois Confederacy and Algonquian language groups.
The Erie Canal’s 1825 completion boosted regional trade along the Mohawk River corridor, yet Clifton Knolls‑Mill Creek remained a quiet farming hamlet for decades.
Clifton Knolls‑Mill Creek is bordered by Clifton Park Center Road to the north, Moe Road to the east, Grooms Road to the south, and Vischer Ferry Road to the west.
